heading: Guidelines
intro: What's appropriate to use Claude for, and what isn't.
do_items: Drafting internal documents, emails, and reports
Summarising long documents or meeting notes
Creating presentation content and speaker notes
Formula help and data work in Excel
dont_items: Entering passwords, credentials, or API keys
Using output in regulated submissions without review
Connecting to finance systems of record
Letting Claude make decisions on your behalf
Sending output to clients without reviewing it
Assuming numerical calculations are always correct
hallucination_intro: Claude can generate confident-sounding content that is factually wrong. This happens with all large language models. It's most likely when Claude generates facts from memory rather than from a document you've provided.

risk_cards.title: Lower risk of errors
risk_cards.body: Summarising, restructuring, and analysing documents you've uploaded. Claude is working from source material you can verify.

risk_cards.title: Higher risk of errors
risk_cards.body: Generating specific statistics, regulatory references, case law, or historical data from memory. Always verify independently.

tips.label: Practical safeguard
tips.body: Ask Claude: "Are you confident in that figure? What's your source?" It will often flag uncertainty if asked. You can also say in your prompt: "If you're not sure about something, say so rather than guessing."

tips.label: Rule of thumb
tips.body: If you wouldn't paste it into an email to a trusted external consultant, think twice before putting it into Claude.
